posts.caption:
🚨 Big news, Second Act community! 🚨
She’s back by popular demand! We are absolutely thrilled to announce that broadcaster, fashion icon, and one of our all-time favourite guests, Jo Good (aka @middleagedminx) , is joining host Ateh Jewel for a special live podcast recording. 🎙️✨
Join us at London’s gorgeous Bankside Hotel for an intimate evening of empowering chat, laughter, and connection. Whether you’re navigating your own second act or just love brilliant conversation with an incredible community, this is the ultimate night out.
✨ What your ticket includes:
Live Podcast & Q&A: Watch the magic happen live and get the chance to ask Ateh and Jo your burning questions.
Bubbles & Bites: Sip on a chilled glass of Prosecco (or a delicious alcohol-free alternative) and enjoy complimentary nibbles.
Exclusive Goodies: Take home a coveted, curated HELLO! goodie bag.
Meet & Greet: Say hello in person! Mix and mingle with both Ateh and Jo and the team during the evening.
✨Date & Time: Monday 29 June📍 Location: Bankside Hotel, London 🎟️ Tickets: Spaces are limited to keep this evening intimate, so grab yours before they sell out!
👉 Click the link in our bio to secure your spot today!
#HELLOSecondAct #JoGood #AtehJewel #MidlifeIcon LivePodcast LondonEvents MidlifeInspiration WomenSupportingWomen

posts.caption:
Who needs a fashion show?⁠
⁠
Korean designer @jiyongkim_ used his Pitti Uomo spotlight as an opportunity to dig into the archive, displaying his archive of gorgeous, one-of-one, sun-bleached garments and wild decomposing @puma sneaker.
posts.alt_text:
@nike Atelier returns with its most football-coded chapter yet, tapping @desire.doue as the face of the Merc Premium collection. ⁠
⁠
The French phenom becomes just the third athlete ever dressed by Nike Atelier. Across a tightly edited lineup of elevated cut-and-sew pieces, the collection is pure football style through a more refined lens.⁠
⁠
The centerpiece, however, is impossible to miss. The Mercurial x Pegasus Premium fuses two icons from Nike’s archive and future, pairing the original 1998 Mercurial upper with the cutting-edge Pegasus Premium sole unit. ⁠
⁠
The collection is slated to be released in July via a list of carefully selected retailers. Swipe for a closer look.

Who needs a fashion show?⁠

While most eyes have been on @skylrk's footwear and apparel, the brand's audio lineup is quietly emerging as one of its most intriguing categories. ⁠
⁠
Spanning sculptural speakers and futuristic headphones, the collection feels less like a side project and more like an extension of SKYLRK's broader design language, where the right aesthetics and tech-forward thinking converge.⁠
⁠
Other than teasing the audio line being teased by Justin Bieber via his own Instagram account, there's no info surrounding an upcoming release. Swipe for a closer look.

posts.caption:
@aliciakeys stepped out in full @pellepelleus for the final night enduring her city’s energy for the Tribeca Film Festival.
Her Knicks jacket was created in collaboration with Allan Houston, paying tribute to the team he played for during his nine seasons in New York. Houston was also a key member of the Knicks’ memorable 1999 NBA Finals run.
“This is really about two brands, FISLL and Pelle Pelle, coming together because we were aligned on our values and the impact we’re committed to making. Pelle Pelle has a longstanding legacy in streetwear and culture, and FISLL is built on purpose, character, and values. Bringing those worlds together felt authentic to both brands”, says Houston on the collaboration.
Closing out a New York festival in unmistakable New York fashion, Keys’ fit says a lot without her even needing to: it’s New York or nowhere.

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.
InStyle Summer, 2026
Editor-in-Chief: @sallyholmes
Writer: @atcodinha
Photographer: @celestesloman
Directors: @ericmichaelroy @benjamingeorgesouth of @cuttingboard.tv
Stylist: @mrfabioimmediato
Hair Stylist: @hairbyadir
Makeup Artist: @jentioseco

posts.alt_text:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum). 

Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood. 

Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.

At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
For @michelleobama, her fashion choices have always been personal, but her recent look has an even deeper meaning.
Last night at a stakeholders event for the Obama Presidential Center, #MichelleObama paid tribute to her late-mother, Marian Robinson, with a custom midi-length sepia-toned pencil skirt from @acnestudios Fall/Winter 2026 collection. The image on the skirt (that was commissioned by her longtime stylist @meredithkoop) Michelle stated was her my "favorite portrait" of her mother and even left her husband @barackobama in tears. Head to the link in bio for more on Michelle and #BarackObama's recent outing.

posts.caption:
Yes, it’s L’OFFICIEL! Cosa hanno in comune “Dune: Part Two”, “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga” e la nuova serie Apple TV “Lucky”? Sono tutti ambientati nel deserto—e tutti vedono protagonista @AnyaTaylorJoy. «Mi diverte sempre, perché non capisco cosa ci sia nei miei capelli, nel mio incarnato o nella larghezza dei miei occhi che mi renda adatta a interpretare una creatura del deserto. Eppure la gente adora mettermi lì», confessa Taylor-Joy a L’OFFICIEL. «Avverto una vera affinità con questi mondi».
Dal pianeta brullo di Arrakis in “Dune” al Wasteland che domina “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga”, la nostra protagonista di giugno continua a ritrovarsi negli scenari più ostili del cinema contemporaneo. «Il deserto possiede una sacralità propria», osserva. Alla domanda su cosa ami di questi spazi desolati, la risposta è immediata: «L’alba e il tramonto, soprattutto. Perché vedi i colori più straordinari e senti di essere l’unica persona sulla terra».
Global Chief Content Officier @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

posts.alt_text:
Un rovescio a una mano, raro nel tennis di oggi, e un gioco elegante e variato: @Lore_Musetti, 24 anni, ha aperto il 2026 conquistando il quinto posto del ranking ATP, prima che un infortunio alla gamba sinistra lo costringesse a rinunciare al Roland Garros. Global ambassador di #BottegaVeneta, il tennista si racconta a @LOfficielItalia, ritratto dagli scatti di @RaffGrosso e con lo styling di @Fernando.Echeverria_, vestendo un total look della collezione #Fall2026, firmato dalla direttrice creativa @Louise_Trotter_. Tra scaramanzie da spogliatoio, il bronzo olimpico di Parigi, l'ammirazione per il tennis di Alcaraz e la passione per la cucina: «Colpire la pallina, il suono che fa quando la colpisco: è questo che mi rende felice». Leggi l’intervista a cura di @Fabia.DiDrusco al link in bio e sul nuovo numero de @LOfficielItalia, ora in edicola.

posts.caption:
46 anni oggi. La nostra cover star @VenusWilliams — nata il 17 giugno 1980 a Lynwood, California — ha cambiato per sempre il tennis e tutto ciò che lo circonda. Sette titoli del Grande Slam, quattro ori olimpici, prima donna afroamericana numero uno al mondo nell’Era Open. Ma anche imprenditrice, designer di moda, attivista per la parità salariale che ottenne lo stesso montepremi per le donne a Wimbledon. Potenza, eleganza, visione — dentro e fuori dal campo. Buon compleanno, Queen V.
Photo: L’OFFICIEL / Ricardo Abrahao
#VenusWilliams #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

posts.caption:
Welcome to the June issue, where L’OFFICIEL explores travel not as movement, but as a state of mind. Our “nomadic by nature” cover star @AnyaTaylorJoy discusses her love of road trips, filming the new Apple TV series ‘Lucky’ in the Las Vegas desert, and her unique approach to learning her characters. The enigmatic photographer @SzilveszterMako, renowned for his surreal images, cinematic backdrops, and dreamlike scenes, turned his lens on the actress, capturing her shifting world for our shoot.
Elsewhere, the issue focuses on slow travel and the art of pausing. We take you to Grand Cayman, Ischia, and Costa Rica, where hydrotherapy and relaxation define the experience, and across the Canadian Rockies, Arabian Desert, and Australian Outback by rail—the defining way to travel in 2026. Elsewhere, we follow one writer into the next frontier of luxury wellness: sleep optimization.
In Paris, we sit down with Hong Kong–born couturier @RobertWun, who has made a name for himself with dramatic, over-the-top silhouettes; he reminds us that slowing down is sometimes the most radical imaginative act. And in Tbilisi, it’s the creative class who are putting the Georgian capital on the map—and we have your guide to everything that’s happening now.
On the fashion front, Spring/Summer 2026’s finest looks head to Cape Town for a sun-drenched escape, while long-heralded summer heroes—unexpected denim and easy dresses—capture the spirit of going nowhere in good company, this time in Los Angeles’s canyons. Stay tuned for our issue to hit newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
Do you want the late #KarlLagerfeld’s pink iPod Touch or his fingerless gloves? @sothebys Paris has announced an auction of Lagerfeld’s personal and work possessions. Unfortunately, his cat, Choupette, is not up for auction—but hundreds of his sketches are.⁠
⁠
Having designed for @ChanelOfficial, @Fendi, and @Chloé, this glimpse into Lagerfeld’s legacy is nothing short of extraordinary. More than 1,000 previously unseen drawings and working documents will be offered in an online sale running July 2–8, providing a rare look into the discipline and creative process that shaped decades of fashion history.⁠
⁠
Alongside the drawings, more than 200 electronics—including Lagerfeld’s famously extensive collection of iPods, which he used to organize music for runway presentations—will also be available. The auction also features personal belongings and mementos that offer a deeper look into the life and legacy of one of fashion’s most influential designers. Visit the link in our bio for more details on the auction.⁠
⁠
Photos courtesy of Sotheby's and Getty Images⁠
⁠
#KarlLagerfeld #Sothebys #KarlLagerfeldDesigner #FashionHistory

posts.caption:
It's been more than three decades since 14-year-old @VenusWilliams first took the tennis world by storm. In the years that followed, the California-born champion—celebrating her 46th birthday today—hasn’t slowed down for a moment.⁠
⁠
“Tennis gave me confidence in myself, in my abilities, and a sense of power that goes beyond the tennis court,” Williams shares. This year marks more than a birthday celebration: Venus and her sister @SerenaWilliams have also received a Wimbledon doubles wild card entry, and will reunite at the tournament where they last claimed a title together a decade ago.⁠
⁠
Wimbledon holds some of Venus’s most significant career memories. As she told L’OFFICIEL, “Being the first woman to receive equal prize money at Wimbledon was a personal milestone for me, and it was a victory for all women in sports and beyond—a reminder that change is possible.”⁠ A five-time Wimbledon singles champion, Venus has also won six Wimbledon women’s doubles titles alongside Serena. At the link in our bio, revisit our interview with Williams as she reflects on her next chapter, what tennis has given her, and what continues to drive her forward.⁠
⁠
Photography @riabrahao⁠
Styled by @luca_falcioni_⁠
⁠
#VenusWilliams #SerenaWilliams #Wimbledon #Wimbledon2026

posts.caption:
Not every escape requires a passport. Tucked into the rugged beauty of the Santa Monica Mountains, fashion finds a welcome reprieve from the pressure to be polished, perfect, and perpetually put together.⁠
⁠
Here, washed denim, breezy prints, and lived-in textures bring a relaxed sensibility to luxury dressing, proving that sophistication doesn't require rigidity. Among winding trails and sun-bleached vistas, even fashion's most refined pieces feel lighter—freed from expectation and transformed by their surroundings.⁠ From @StellaMcCartney and @Chanelofficial to @ysl and @MiuMiu, we're taking luxury off-road. See how the season's standout pieces navigate the Santa Monica Mountains at the link in bio.⁠ ⁠

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
Taylor Swift’s latest jewelry choice is putting Jaipur craftsmanship on the global stage.
The singer was recently spotted wearing a ruby and diamond ring from Jaipur-based Amrapali Jewels, one of India’s most celebrated luxury jewelry houses. Featuring a striking ruby surrounded by rose-cut diamonds, the piece reflects the artistry and gemstone heritage that have made Jaipur a global center for fine jewelry.
For Indian luxury, it’s another reminder that homegrown craftsmanship is increasingly finding its place on some of the world’s biggest stages.
📷: @taylorswift @amrapalijewels

From Louis Vuitton and Hermès to Prada, Chanel, Cartier, Tiffany & Co., and Rolls-Royce, these extraordinary chess sets combine exceptional craftsmanship, premium materials, and distinctive design, proving that even a game board can become a statement of luxury.
Swipe through to discover 10 of the most luxurious chess sets ever created.

The FIFA World Cup has become far more than a football tournament. It is now one of the biggest business machines in global sport.
The 2026 edition is expected to generate a record $13 billion in revenue through broadcasting rights, sponsorships, ticket sales, and hospitality, making it the most lucrative World Cup in history. As the tournament continues to expand, so does its impact on the global sports economy.
#Luxurious

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra)
Interview: Akshay Kaushal (@akshay_kaushal)
Photography: Vaishnav Praveen (@vaishnavpraveen)
Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)
Video: Kris Arshad (@itskrisarshad)
PR Agency: Tree-Shul Media (@treeshulmediasolutions)
Artist management: Benchmark Talents (@benchmarktalents)
Production: Studio Little Dumpling (@studiolittledumpling)
On Rukmini: pantsuit, Kanika Goyal Label (@kanikagoyallabel); earrings, bangles, and rings, Gehna Jewellers (@gehnajewellers1)

#Manifest #manifestcover #rukminivasanth
posts.alt_text:
Indian fashion labels that are now redefining sustainability by giving old textiles, heirloom saris, and fabric waste a second life. Instead of creating new materials, these brands work with what already exists—repairing, restoring, reweaving, and transforming discarded fabrics into contemporary clothing and accessories.

The common thread across all five labels is the belief that sustainability isn’t about constantly buying “eco-friendly” products—it’s about extending the life of what already exists. Whether through mending, restoration, upcycling, or reinvention, these designers are proving that India’s long-standing culture of reuse can be both luxurious and contemporary.

In other words: your grandmother’s silk sari, a forgotten heirloom, or even textile scraps could have an entire second chapter waiting to be written.

Read more to link in bio🔗

By Mirza Shaina Beg (@mirzashainaa)

posts.caption:
Hollywood actress Sophie Tuner rocked a pair at Priyanka Chopra’s wedding and they’re also a fave with the stars from Alia Bhatt to Deepika Padukone!
At Indian shaadis, too, the jutti sure grabs the spotlight. From pastel pre-wedding functions to sparkling sangeet nights and breezy honeymoon getaways, this is the accessory that makes the smooth transition.
Think peach-pink hues, elegant pearls and embroidery, mirrors that meet zari, beads on silk and more. We’ve rounded up the ultimate jutti board to bookmark for every kind of bride.

posts.alt_text:
#ManifestCover 

There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.

For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.

“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.

Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”

Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.

Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

posts.caption:
There’s one little accessory that carries tradition, style and takes a page out of history. The nath or nose ring, is that adornment. From royal courts to bridal traditions and prominently in Indian weddings, and modern fashion, the nath has travelled through centuries while remaining relevant across different regions of India.
Maharashtra has its iconic Peshwai nath, Uttarakhand its maximalist nathuli, while Bengal is known for its delicate floral styles.
Whether it’s an heirloom passed down through generations or a modern version picked for your big day, it carries stories, memories and a sense of belonging with it.. We explore its history, cultural significance, regional styles and the stories, memories and sense of belonging it carries at the link in bio.🔗
By Kratagya Rathore (@kratagya_rathore)
[Bridal Fashion, Traditional Jewellery, Regional and Cultural Significance]

posts.caption:
Your favourite newlywed couple doesn’t need another gift that’s going to live in its box forever.
They’re entering their “we live together now” era—complete with half-unpacked boxes, mismatched cushions, a growing collection of fridge magnets, and heated debates over where the coffee table should go.
The best wedding gifts are the ones that become part of their everyday story—the dinner set that hosts their first dinner party, the candle holder that lights up cosy date nights, the rug that witnesses lazy Sunday mornings, and the bedspread they’ll never stop getting compliments on.
If you’re shopping for your friends or relatives setting up their first home together, skip the predictable and gift them something they’ll actually use and love. From luxury home décor and statement tableware to thoughtful home essentials, these newlywed gifts are equal parts practical and beautiful.
Because the best wedding gifts don’t just fill a home—they help make it feel like one.
Cover Image: Sabyasachi (@sabyasachiofficial) for Nilaya @worldofnilaya
Curated by: Mirza Shaina Beg (@mirzashainaa)
#Manifest #Gifts
[Wedding Gifts, Luxury Home Decor, Gift Guide, Housewarming]

posts.caption:
Priyanka Chopra Jonas (@priyankachopra) continues to show us that one can do it all, and do it on their own terms.
For an entire generation, she’s been the blueprint for dreaming bigger. From winning Miss World in 2000 to breaking barriers in Hollywood, producing meaningful stories through Purple Pebble Pictures, championing children’s rights with UNICEF and building a truly global career, Priyanka has spent decades proving that ambition has no postcode.
And now, she’s added another jewel to her crown.
Rolex has officially welcomed Priyanka Chopra Jonas to its prestigious family of Testimonees, recognising not just her achievements, but her influence as a cultural force who continues to redefine what success looks like on a global stage.
Honestly, it feels like a fitting match. Excellence, longevity, precision and timeless impact—qualities Rolex is known for, and qualities Priyanka has embodied every step of the way. She’s definitely served up another well-timed ace, quite literally!

posts.caption:
Last week, Ellie the Elephant, beloved mascot of the New York Liberty, celebrated her sixth birthday party. It was a bash involving dance routines and costume changes, held in front of 17,000 people at Barclays Center. Many elements of Ellie’s birthday outfit and party were provided by sponsors. But to her fans, Ellie is far more than a billboard.
Over the past six years, Ellie’s distinctive aesthetic and courtside panache have allowed many Black women, Liberty fans or not, to see themselves in her. In performance, she has been known to channel divas like Mary J. Blige, Lil’ Kim and Whitney Houston. “Ellie represents culture,” Criscia Long, senior director of entertainment for the Liberty, told the New York Times reporter @s_evangelina.
At the link in our bio, see more scenes from @bigellieliberty’s birthday bash and read more about what the mascot means to fans. Visuals by @djdumpling
posts.alt_text:
A reader asked our fashion critic: "I’m 52 years old. During my life, jeans fashion has gone from bell bottoms to acid-wash to ripped to boot-cut to low-rise to skinny to high-rise to mom jeans to ripped again to wide-leg. … Please make it stop. I don’t have another jeans transition left in me. Is there a timeless jeans style for those of us who simply cannot keep up?"

The options for denim can be overwhelming. At the link in our bio, @vvfriedman offers advice on how to choose the style that works best for you. Photo by Getty Images

posts.caption:
The most charming team outfit we’ve seen thus far at the World Cup did not derive from some sportswear juggernaut with a billion-dollar ad budget. Instead, it was the product of a self-taught 30-year-old whose brand has fewer than 10,000 Instagram followers.
Alvin Mak, a Congolese designer in Paris, is the man responsible for the leopard-embellished suits and leopard bags that the Congolese national team wore for its arrival in Houston last week. The team, which is appearing in its first World Cup since 1974, doesn’t play its first match until Wednesday, but it has already made quite an impression. Images of the team in their outfits spread broadly online, drawing comments like “when can I purchase those bags” in multiple languages.
Mak hadn’t expected such an outsize, global response and is still catching up to all the attention. “In Congo culture, the spirit of leopard is a spirit of strength,” @alvin_jmak told our reporter @jacobwgallagher. “It is the spirit of resilience, so I want to transfer this energy to them.”
At the link in our bio, learn how the Congolese team's @jmakxparis outfits came together. Photos by Troy Taormina/Imagn Images, via Reuters, Maria Lysaker/Imagn Images, via Reuters

posts.caption:
Last year, the designer Mike Amiri encountered the soccer effect for the first time.
F.C. Barcelona, the Spanish soccer team that has won the Champions League five times and is contributing 16 players to various World Cup teams, announced that Amiri would be its official formal wear partner for the next five years. The result of the announcement, Amiri said, trumped the effect of any of the celebrities — like Justin Bieber, Teyana Taylor and Maluma — who wear his label.
“We were a niche Los Angeles brand, doing shows in Paris, and then all of a sudden we became global,” Amiri said. Athletes like Travis Kelce, Kyle Kuzma and Odell Beckham Jr. have gravitated toward the label, not only because of the fit of the clothes, but also because Amiri took an interest in sports stars when the fashion industry ignored them.
Now, as the World Cup begins and fashion wises up to the ways that soccer can elevate brands, Amiri may be a case study in what happens next. Tap the link in our bio to read more about how @amiri is leveling up. Photos by @theirnameisricardo, Amiri, Chris Graythen/Getty Images

At the first U.S. home World Cup match against Paraguay in Los Angeles on Friday, the colors and the chants were loud with both nations’ affinities for red and white stripes on display. There was also a red, white and blue Liberty flame hat, some sombreros, and at least one pair of fans dressed in Stars and Stripes overalls.
Tap the link in our bio to see more moments of creative expression at the match. Photos by @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
David Hockney, the artist who died on Thursday at 88, had a madcap maximalism in his approach to style. As with his paintings, he favored bold colors for his clothes.
"It always seemed as if there were another Hockney outfit to discover," the fashion reporter Jacob Gallagher wrote. His style pinged around the mood boards of the fashion world for decades, showing the painter at every life stage: the young Londoner in his pink-striped rugby shirt and pulverized plimsolls, or the still whimsical Pop Art icon in mod tweed suits well into his 80s. Hockney became a north star for designers trying to get men to wear more color.
"When it comes to style, some people just have it," @jacobwgallagher wrote. "It seems to emanate from their very being. Hockney was that kind of man." Read more about Hockney's style legacy and see more photos at the link in our bio. Photos by @nathanael.turner, Anthony Barboza/Getty Images, Aaron Chown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images, @suzanne_plunkett

posts.caption:
I fear #ChristianLouboutin has taken the whimsy trend to a whole new level, transforming one of the most iconic visual gags (the banana peel slip) into a shoe.
Made of yellow satin, the $1,995 heels feature hand-sculpted leather panels which curl around the foot to resemble the folds of a peeled banana. The design is a homage to the footwear of #IsabelCanovas who became renowned for transforming fruit, vegetables, flowers, shells, and other everyday objects into wearable sculptures. Much like her creations, the #Louboutin heel elevates something ordinary and  slightly absurd into luxury. #Shoes #FootwearDesign
Images courtesy of @styledbyjovon_ @christianlouboutin & The Metropolitan Museum of Art
posts.alt_text:
#Balenciaga Spring 2027 may be #PierpaoloPiccioli’s clearest vision for the brand yet. Less about direct references, it focuses on #CristóbalBalenciaga’s philosophy of construction and the relationship between fabric and the body.

References to the house’s historic balloon, drape, and cocoon shapes suggest a welcome return to its heritage, but reengineered for modern life. Through new textile innovations, the collection emphasises lightness, with entire looks weighing less than a kilogram. @pppiccioli wants to demonstrate how #HauteCouture principles can be relevant to everyday life. Ordinary wardrobe staples are elevated through couture-level construction.

What do you think of the new Balenciaga so far?

posts.caption:
If you have any of these bags, please step forward… #KarlLagerfeld was known for his larger-than-life shows, and the spectacle didn’t just stop at the sets, but translated into his absurd yet highly collectible bags.
Think of the iconic hula hoop bag from Spring 2013, which Karl joked was designed for the beach so you could stick it into the sand and hang your towel from it (which fyi someone is currently selling on eBay for £145K.) Then there was the supermarket show, complete with milk carton bags and the #Chanel chain basket, or the Spring 2019 flap bag filled with sand.
The bags became souvenirs from those fantasy worlds and now #MatthieuBlazy is continuing that tradition, turning handbags into collectible objects that blur the line between fashion and art.
Which one is your favourite?

posts.caption:
First debuted for Fall 2024, the Robe Spirale was a feat of fashion engineering. At its core was a 3D-printed structure formed as one continuous coiled strip. Like a slap bracelet, it wrapped and snapped around the torso, using the natural curves of the body to hold itself securely in place.
#PieterMulier further developed the idea for Spring 2025 with a series of pleated dresses that appeared to float on the body. They gripped the torso at carefully calculated points, while hidden internal armatures carried much of the structural load. The visible fabric acted as the skin of the construction, and the lightweight pleating reduced the need for heavy internal support.
Just like #AzzedineAlaïa, @pieter_mulier obsesses with the idea of construction and rhe body merging fashion, sculpture and architecture engineering.

posts.caption:
This has to be one of my favourite editorial shoots I've seen this year, and that's probably because it was shot by #SzilveszterMako, whose images feel closer to theatre and painting than traditional fashion photography.
His editorials often have a dreamlike quality because of their painterly approach. Through dramatic lighting, strong contrasts between light and shadow he gives his subjects an almost sculptural presence. The clothing blends seamlessly into the composition, contributing to the overall mood and becoming an extension of the subject rather than something just being worn.
#Rihanna shot by @szilvesztermako for @ee72, styled by @edward_enninful wearing #Schiaparelli #Chanel #Dior Alexander McQueen & Bottega Veneta

posts.caption:
One of my favourite, and most hauntingly beautiful details from #DanielRoseberry’s #Schiaparelli couture collections was the sheer crinoline veils/face masks, from Fall 2024. Wrapped around the face and head they functioned as sculptural forms. The material appeared almost weightless, creating distortions that blurred the contours of the face and transformed the models into embryonic works of art. Makeup by @patstingz, hair by @guidopalau
Also reminded me of #GarethPugh’s Spring 2013 collection, which featured sheer mesh and stretched fabrics wrapped around the head and face, or #MarcJacobs Spring 2019. Major inspo for brides looking for an unconventional wedding veil!

Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness
posts.alt_text:
FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more

posts.caption:
Conveying stories simply is boring; Mukta Dhond prefers adding some masala in life. In her childhood, she would wrap every single thing with drama, rather than saying it directly. After studying journalism, Mukta realised that expressing emotions was better than telling the truth.
Today, she is a seasoned producer, director and writer in the Indian television industry, with a strong track record of creating stories that resonate deeply with audiences. Mukta crafts narratives that are rooted in emotion, relationships, and cultural relevance.
A single mother, Mukta Dhond, continues to shape contemporary Indian television with stories that are not just entertaining but also connecting, which makes her an influential voice in the industry.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full interview by @_shivanilamba
#divyadrishti #television #drama #content Mukta Dhond, writer, director, storytelling, emotions, drama, expressions, television industry, stories, relationships, fantasy, love stories, entertainment

posts.caption:
Anjali Sivaraman - Actor, Model, Musician & Cat Mom - chooses characters that go against the grain of what women are "supposed" to be like. "Society often tries to put women into a mould but we are so layered," said @anjalisivaraman, speaking to SheThePeople about female representation in cinema.
Anjali has consistently chosen roles that are bold, rebellious, and often rough around the edges. The fauji kid-turned-artiste admits that she brings a tinge of her real-life badass personality into her characters.
Anjali is best known for her roles in 'Bad Girl', directed by Varsha Bharath, and the Netflix suspense-drama series 'Class'. She is set to star in Amrita Sher-Gil's biopic 'Amri' by Mira Nair.
Full interview by Tanya Savkoor coming soon on www.shethepeople.tv | Stay tuned!
#anjalisivaraman #bollywood #webseries bollywood movies Hindi web series Hindi movies Tamil movies Tamil films Tamil cinema Hindi cinema feminist films feminist movies feminist stories

posts.caption:
The World Cup soon ‘kicks’ off. And soon, streets and sofas in front of televisions or people planted on chairs in restaurants will be adorned with unique jerseys and even more unique celebration methods.
Today, it’s hard to spot a single fan who’s not wearing a jersey while a football game is on. However, it no longer remains confined to the limits of a stadium. It’s a statement. Oftentimes, people are more than happy to wear dupes of a team’s jersey they couldn’t recognise with a magnifying glass. Nevertheless, an authentic jersey is equivalent to a prized heirloom.
However, when was it that this jersey-mania became prevalent? Like every trend, there isn’t a singular answer but a journey to take you through.
Opinion: @the_haute_historian | Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more
#fifaworldcup2026 #football #jersey #fashion
Football jerseys, fashion, Football players, Dua lipa, Bella Hadid, stadiums

posts.caption:
Unpopular opinion: the wedding day is the easiest part of wedding planning.
The hard part? The six months before it. The follow-ups. The changing guest lists. The vendor coordination. The approvals. The countless decisions nobody sees. Hundreds of conversations, dozens of revisions, thousands of tiny details - all happening quietly behind the scenes.
Because a smooth wedding day isn’t luck. It’s preparation. By the time the wedding day arrives, the real work has already been done - the big day is simply the RESULT of everything that came before it. That’s where Chekhmate steps in, giving planners one place to manage follow-ups, vendor coordination and approvals, so the months before the wedding feel just as smooth as the day itself. ♟️✨

posts.caption:
Some weddings are celebrations. And some are coronations.
Prachi and Diego’s wedding at Chomu Palace felt like the latter, where centuries-old courtyards, sweeping archways, and the gold-kissed light of Rajasthan came together to witness two families, two cultures, becoming one.
There was a quiet majesty to it all. The kind that didn’t need to be announced, it simply unfolded: in the swirl of a lehenga catching the afternoon sun, in joined hands walking through halls that have seen royalty before, and were, that day, witnessing it again.
This wasn’t just a wedding. It was a love story given a palace to live in, if only for one day. 👑
Captured exactly as it unfolded, the happy tears, the bear hugs, the stolen smiles, and the joy of two families coming together for one unforgettable celebration.
Couple: @prachixdiego, @diegofvilchez, @explore_with_pm
Venue: Chomu Palace, Jaipur
Wedding Planner: @balajiaffairs
Makeup Artist: @bridebyy_ashmieta
Outfit: @frontierraas
Invites: @illustrationpalette_
Photos Courtesy: @happyframes_
#Justchekhmateit

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
The things a fashion stylist carries that no one ever sees.
The quiet fear you’ve plateaued. Watching work you wanted go to someone else, and being
happy for them while it still stings. The guilt of wanting more when you already have jobs others
would kill for. Never feeling quite ready to reach for the dream clients you can clearly see.
Here’s what no one tells you. None of that means you’ve peaked. It means you’ve outgrown
waiting to be chosen. Your dream clients were never going to find you, you go to them. And
naming the work you want isn’t ungrateful. It’s the first real move toward booking it.
Save this if you’ve carried any of it. And send it to a stylist who has too.
Are you booking your dream clients?

You’re waiting to feel ready. A better portfolio, a bit more experience, a few more followers, then you’ll reach out. But that day never quite comes, does it.
Here’s the thing no one says: your dream client isn’t out there looking for you. Not because you’re not good enough. They’re just busy running their own world.
So you have to go first. Every stylist booking the work you want stopped waiting a while ago.
They didn’t get discovered. They reached out. It started with a list of ten names, and one real message to one real person.
That’s the whole thing you’ve been afraid of.
Save this for the next time you catch yourself waiting. And send it to a fashion stylist who needs
the push.

posts.caption:
Celebrity make-up artists in India have been shaping faces, trends and bridal moodboards for years. The beauty boom is here. The artist-led empire is still missing.
Globally, names like Charlotte Tilbury, Pat McGrath and Bobbi Brown turned artistry into authority, and authority into billion-dollar beauty businesses. In India, the same conversion has been slower. Local artists have built trust across skin tones, textures, climates and undertones, often doing the kind of real-world R&D global brands ignored for years.
They know what oxidises on brown skin. They know what collapses in Mumbai humidity. They know what survives a wedding, a shoot and a close-up.
Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics
posts.alt_text:
Celebrating the rise of the Godawan began with a simple question: how does a bird return to public memory?

A bird that builds its nest directly on the ground is especially vulnerable when people keep taking over its habitat. Once considered a candidate for India’s national bird, the Great Indian Bustard, locally known as Godawan, has gradually disappeared from public consciousness over the decades due to habitat loss and human encroachment.

Today, its conservation story is gaining renewed attention. In Rajasthan, breeding and hatchery programmes have brought cautious progress, with new hatchlings emerging in protected facilities. Conservation efforts combine habitat restoration, protected nesting areas, water management, predator control, captive breeding, and community-led monitoring of breeding grounds.

On World Environment Day, one of India’s most iconic monuments became a canvas for this conservation story, as Qutb Minar hosted projection mapping that highlighted the Godawan’s ecological role in India’s grassland ecosystems and the challenges facing its survival, with support from Godawan Estuary Premium Water.

posts.caption:
Solo dining in India still comes with unsolicited concern. People assume you’ve been stood up, abandoned by friends, or are being brave in a way nobody asked for.
But younger Indians are changing the script. Gen Z and millennials are showing up alone at restaurants, runs, quizzes, food walks, gigs and climbing walls. At Bangalore Climbing Initiatives, nearly 80 per cent of people come solo. Restaurants are adding counter seating and smaller formats so a table for one feels less like a logistical problem.
The solo hang is no longer just about being alone. It is about choosing your time, your interests and the people you might meet along the way.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang ( @kkuenzang)
#solodining #solodates #urbanloneliness #genzindia #thirdspaces

posts.caption:
Depression is often described through what it takes away: energy, sleep, appetite, motivation and a sense of self. Far less often is it discussed through the lens of intimacy.
A reduced sex drive is one of the most common experiences associated with depression, yet it remains largely absent from conversations around mental health. For many people, the loss of desire arrives alongside emotional exhaustion, physical fatigue and the daily effort of simply getting through the day. The challenges can continue even after treatment begins, with antidepressants carrying sexual side effects that many patients are unaware of until they experience them.
The impact extends beyond the individual, shaping relationships, communication and self-perception in ways that are rarely acknowledged. As therapist Aanandita Vagahani puts it: “It’s often one of the first things to go and one of the last things to come back—and that has enormous consequences for relationships and self-worth.”
Read more about how depression and sex reshape intimacy by Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia) at the link in bio
#Depression #MentalHealth #SexualHealth #IntimacyMatters #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Skincare has moved from bottles to jars and from serums to supplements. Peptide supplements are now headlining the edible skincare era, promising beauty from within.
But dermatologists caution: while oral collagen peptides show modest improvements in hydration and wrinkles, the claims around hair growth and miracle fixes are overstated.
The bigger story? Supplements are becoming lifestyle products, consumed by 70% of Indians without doctor consultation, driven by marketing that often blurs science.
The future of beauty may be edible, but it’s also a reminder to question the hype.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#Peptides #Supplements #SkinHealth #CollagenPeptides #BeautyCulture

posts.caption:
I’m an editor first, but I’m also an interiors girl. That combo means there are very few things I enjoy more than a good showroom, and @realisationpar’s flagship at 95 Mount Street might be my favorite place in London…to shop, at least.
The new flagship feels like stepping into someone’s very well-lived-in, very well-dressed apartment. Designed as a collaboration between founders Alexandra Spencer and Teale Talbot and designer Sophie Baker, the space leans into this idea of a “sensory archive” rather than traditional retail.
Upstairs, it’s all soft light, antique furniture, and layered textures while downstairs, a dressing-room-meets-private-salon with mirrored walls, rich fabrics, and just the right amount of drama.
You’ve got to stop by.
#RealisationPar #LondonShopping #RetailDesign #FashionRetail #StoreDesign
posts.alt_text:
I’m not sure @simon_porte_jacquemus has ever missed when it comes to runway locations, but this might be one of his best yet. For Spring/Summer 2027, @jacquemus will present “Le Bonheur” at Phare de la Pietra, a historic lighthouse perched on a rocky island off the coast of Île-Rousse in Corsica.
It marks a return to those sweeping, site-specific natural landscapes the brand does so well.

The show will take place June 29 at 10 a.m. local time, so be ready to tune in on Instagram if your invitation got lost in the mail.

And it goes deeper than just the visuals: Jacquemus is hosting Corsica-wide castings to feature local models and partnering with the town to help restore the lighthouse.

A runway, but also a gesture.

#Jacquemus #RunwayShow #FashionWeek #SS27 #FashionCulture

I’m an editor first, but I’m also an interiors girl. That combo means there are very few things I enjoy more than a good showroom, and @realisationpar’s flagship at 95 Mount Street might be my favorite place in London…to shop, at least.

The new flagship feels like stepping into someone’s very well-lived-in, very well-dressed apartment. Designed as a collaboration between founders Alexandra Spencer and Teale Talbot and designer Sophie Baker, the space leans into this idea of a “sensory archive” rather than traditional retail.

Upstairs, it’s all soft light, antique furniture, and layered textures while downstairs, a dressing-room-meets-private-salon with mirrored walls, rich fabrics, and just the right amount of drama.

You’ve got to stop by.

#RealisationPar #LondonShopping #RetailDesign #FashionRetail #StoreDesign

posts.caption:
Good afternoon to everyone successfully surviving the week so far. Whether you’re touching grass after this past weekend’s New York Knicks win or leaning fully into your inner art enthusiast, there’s objectively too much happening at once right now. Men’s Fashion Week is about to kick off in Milan and Paris ahead of couture season in July, the World Cup is already underway, and apparently everyone is in love.
Zendaya and Tom Holland are back on the red carpet together, Dua Lipa and Callum Turner are honeymooning somewhere impossibly chic, and now Sarah Pidgeon has been spotted with Joe Alwyn in NYC.
It’s fine. We’re fine. I’m fine.
Happy Tuesday.
#FashionWeek #WorldCup #Zendaya #DuaLipa #LoveStory

posts.caption:
Shut down the internet because the undisputed royalty of the red carpet has officially returned. Nearly five years after their last major joint appearance for Spider-Man: No Way Home, Zendaya and Tom Holland stepped out in Madrid to kick off the press tour for Spider-Man: Brand New Day—and naturally, they look amazing.
Styled by Law Roach, Zendaya wore a custom Christian Cowan black mini dress with a sculpted sweetheart neckline and web-like tasseled detailing. Holland kept things classic but coordinated in a relaxed crimson shirt layered under sharp black tailoring.
Very polished. Very intentional. Very on-theme.
Images: Backgrid
#Zendaya #TomHolland #SpiderMan #ChristianCowan #LawRoach

posts.caption:
Rule of thumb: never skip a Paul Smith campaign. Walton Goggins recently returned as the face of Paul Smith for AW26, this time getting a crash course in sartorial etiquette from Sir Paul himself.
The campaign unfolds across four short films directed by Cameron Turnbull, with stills by Samuel Bradley, set against the British countryside. Styled by Ben Schofield, it leans into everything @paulsmithdesign does best: heritage fabrics like Harris Tweed and Donegal wool, offset with just enough wit to keep it from feeling too proper.
Tie rules, the sock dilemma in shoes-off households, the “Sometimes, Always, Never” jacket principle—it’s all there, ending with Sir Paul stepping in to inspect the final look.
#PaulSmith #WaltonGoggins #FashionCampaign #MenswearStyle #Tailoring

posts.caption:
Bring me here ASAP. Built between 1902 and 1930, Lluís Domènech i Montaner designed Hospital de la Santa Creu i Sant Pau around a radical idea for its time: that sunlight, gardens, color, and beautiful architecture could actively help heal the human body.
While a modern hospital still operates next door, the original Art Nouveau pavilions have since become a UNESCO World Heritage Site: a sprawling “garden city” of tiled facades, mosaic details, palm-lined pathways, and stained glass flooded with natural light.
A genuinely therapeutic place to spend an afternoon.
#Barcelona #ArtNouveau #Architecture #Travel #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Every town has a story it tells about itself, and on Apple TV’s ‘Widow’s Bay’, that story involves myths, ghosts, a masked killer known only as the Boogeyman, and an immortal founding father. Led by Matthew Rhys as the town’s well-meaning mayor, Tom Loftis, the show is already being talked about as the best of the year.
Every breakout show has a character that captures its fans’ imagination more than others, and on Widow’s Bay the competition is stiff. So, it’s no small feat that Patricia, played with masterful precision by Kate O’Flynn, has emerged as the one character that audiences can’t stop simultaneously worrying about, laughing at, and rooting for. In the same way that Stephen Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ belongs to Courtney Grace, the internet’s consensus is that ‘Widow’s Bay’ increasingly feels like Patricia’s show.
At the link in bio, @arushi491 on why, in a town full of ghosts, killers, and ancient curses, the slightly embarrassing character is also the final girl to rule them all.

posts.caption:
Rahul Khanna has been breaking the internet with his thirst traps. His IG feed is chock-full of pumped-up arms in muscle tees, shirtless selfies taken lounging by a pool, post-shower, or mid-run. But like everything about him, his barely clad photographs are also polished and tasteful—even when he’s in nothing but a towel.
Naturally, each image gets a reaction but Mr Khanna (as he’s known on Instagram) tries not to look at his DMs. “Sometimes, they can get a little weird. But I love it when people are inspired to be witty, cheeky, and creative with their comments. I will often jump in and banter or engage in a pun battle with a playful follower,” he admits.
At the link in bio, @dandydujour chats with the the internet’s crush about shirtless selfies, taking style risks, and the one thing he’ll gatekeep forever.

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.caption:
Every culture created a goddess who presided over making, but not all of them did it the same way.
This edition introduced two categories.
The first: goddesses whose mythology is explicitly about weaving (documented, primary sources, this is what the texts actually say).
The second: goddesses that weavers chose: figures of creativity, wisdom and skill that women at the loom reached toward deliberately, even though weaving wasn’t the centre of their story.
Both categories are true, just in different ways.
And here is what I keep coming back to across all three editions of the First Makers series. You never find a GOD of weaving. Not once, across any culture. Every time, the act of making cloth belongs to the feminine divine.
That is the oldest memory we have of what women’s knowledge meant.
Full episode on The Culture of Cloth podcast 🎧 link in bio. Start there if you’re new here.
Save this and share it with someone who makes things.
Cover art: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk, a Chinese silk painting by Emperor Huizong of Song, early 12th century.

posts.caption:
Six months of research, four in-depth online sessions and one of the oldest stories ever told 📕
I’m joining @readingsbooks Big Book Club for a deep dive into Homer’s Odyssey. It’s an online session running every Thursday from 18 June through to the Christopher Nolan film screening on 16 July.
Penelope. Athena. Circe. Helen. Nausicaa. These women have been living in my research for months and this series is the moment I get to go deeper with scholars who’ve spent their careers inside this text.
I’ll be sharing Substack reflections after each session and the first one is free, with sessions 2–5 are for paid subscribers.
Link in bio for the Readings online membership.
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption: For decades, Indian artisans have quietly shaped European luxury, crafting the embroideries and textiles behind some of couture’s most iconic houses. This season marks a shift: more Indian designers than ever are on the official Paris calendar, with @ManishMalhotra05 set to make his Paris Haute Couture Week debut for @ManishMalhotraWorld on July 8 as the fourth Indian designer to feature on the schedule. @Sujstyle explores what these signals of change mean at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: South Korean designer @Jiyong_Kim made his European debut for @JiyongKim_Official at @PittiUomo_Official today. Speaking with @AshleyOgawaClarke ahead of his Pitti guest appearance, the designer shares what the trade show means for the brand and how his innovative sun-bleaching faces challenges when it comes to scale. “We’ve been dreaming about this a lot since the very beginning. It’s an opportunity for us to present our collection in a new way outside Seoul,” says Kim. Tap the link in bio for more details on the fabric technique capturing industry attention.

posts.caption:
Social media has hit a roadblock. This week, the UK government announced a social media ban for under-16s, effective next year. It’s the latest sign that the anti-social media movement is reaching a tipping point, as the push to limit children’s exposure to addictive algorithms and other potential online harms extends beyond activist interests into the mainstream.
Brands and marketers will need to adapt to how they reach these consumers, likely resulting in a much more fragmented marketing strategy. Experts say multiplayer games like Roblox and Fortnite — where under-16s are socializing more — could become an appealing alternative for brands. Tap the link in bio for a detailed history of social media.
Photo: @ghostindress

posts.caption: Last week, Hugo Boss’s largest shareholder, Frasers Group, announced plans to launch a takeover bid. The timing is notable. @Boss is no longer the brand Daniel Grieder inherited in 2021: sales have doubled and the company has moved into the next phase of its Claim 5 strategy, focused on building brand equity and improving profitability. If the deal goes ahead, the question is not whether Hugo Boss needs a new strategy — but whether full @FrasersGroup ownership would help complete the one already underway. @KirstyMcGregorEdits has all the details at the link in bio.

Long before F1 drivers became fashion week regulars and luxury-brand ambassadors, they were already walking in style off the track. From James Hunt’s rockstar appeal to Pierre Gasly’s love for contemporary streetwear, Formula 1 has always produced racers with influence far beyond the paddock. If we missed any of your favourites, tell us in the comments below. Full list at the link in bio.
Image credits: @gettyimages
#VogueFashion

Priyanka Chopra (@priyankachopra) was recently announced as Rolex’s (@rolex) newest brand ambassador. A common theme among the watch company, is the remarkable women who wear it. posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text:
At Café Rue Dix, a Senegalese-French restaurant in Brooklyn, the World Cup is about more than football. “It’s so much more than sports,” says the restaurant’s co-owner Nilea Alexander. “It’s politics, it’s fashion, it’s triumph, it’s so much.” Though the café is not a sports bar, it shows the Africa Cup of Nations games, and this year the couple couldn’t resist screening the thrilling NBA Finals too. Go inside their screening party for France vs. Senegal at the link in our bio.

Photographed by @poupayphoto

posts.caption:
Olympian @Mondo_Duplantis and model @DesireInglander first connected in midsummer 2020. After their first date, their relationship grew and the pair became inseparable. They got engaged in October 2024, and waited until spring 2025 to dig into the wedding planning, but there was one thing they knew for sure: the location. “We already knew that we wanted a wedding in the South of France since we have spent a lot of time there before at my mom and her husband’s house,” notes Desiré.
When it came to wedding day fashion, the bride knew she wanted to wear a Swedish designer to exchange vows. She chose @IdaLanto_Couture, who she had worked with on gala looks with over the years. “We started a year before, and she made my vision come to life,” Desiré shares. Mondo opted for a white jacket and black pants for the ceremony. “@SamanAmel worked with me to create my perfect wedding day look,” he says. Head to the link in our bio for more details on the newlyweds' celebration in the South of France.
Photos: @GregFinck

posts.caption: The “Spider-man: Brand New Day” tour is a special one, given it marks @Zendaya and Tom Holland’s first splashy appearance together as husband and wife and we now have our first glimpse at their married couple style. For the film’s Amsterdam photocall Holland looked dapper in his sleek @ThomBrowne suiting while Zendaya stunned in @louisvuitton. See all of their press looks thus far at the link in bio.

Out and about in New York City for her "LUX" tour, the singer made it clear she will back balletcore until the bitter end. Tap the link in our bio for details on her daytime fashions in the city.

posts.caption: @MichelleObama is keeping her late mother’s memory alive through her clothing. Today, the former first lady joined her husband, former President Barack Obama, for an address to stakeholders at the Barack Obama Presidential Center in Chicago, Illinois. For the occasion, Obama chose to pay homage to her mother, Marian Robinson in a custom Acne Studios pencil skirt bearing a sepia portrait of her mother as a young woman. Tap the link in our bio for more on Obama's meaningful fashion choice.

posts.caption: Italy may be out of contention at the World Cup, but one thing that they’re certainly in is fashion month. The spring 2027 menswear season kicks off in Florence with the Pitti Uomo fair, a sprawling tradeshow held at Fortezza da Basso, where brands present their latest wares to peacocking dandies. This time around, Dover Street Market’s Kei Ninomiya is one of the guest designers alongside Simone Rocha, who will be showcasing her first solo menswear show. See more of the best street style from this season at the link in bio. Photographed by @styledumone
posts.alt_text: A giant teddy bear kept watch over @MaxMara’s 75th-anniversary celebrations at Shanghai’s Long Museum. The supersized mascot was a tribute to the teddy coat, the brand’s fluffy phenomenon, which, alongside the immortal 101801 camel coat, has wrapped countless women in a cocoon of practical chic. “Max Mara is fundamentally about city life,” said Ian Griffith in a preview. “Women navigating work, leisure, and everything in between. So for a collection built around urban energy, Shanghai felt inevitable.” See every look and read the full review at the link in bio.

posts.caption: What did street style look like a decade ago? In the lead-up to the spring 2017 men’s season, we’re time traveling back 10 years to see what dapper guys were wearing as they headed to and from the shows. Tap the link in bio to see more. Photographed by @mrstreetpeeper

posts.caption:
Eight years after his first tenure at @Celine, where he served as design director under Phoebe Philo, Michael Rider returned to the house—and in the time since, has delivered three acclaimed collections. His coveted spring line is in stores, and his fall collection received a rapturous reception when it was shown in March. Rider has certainly put a stop to any lingering questions as to how a curious kid from Washington, DC, ended up running one of the most quintessentially Parisian of fashion houses. Head to the link in bio for more on how Rider is remaking the French label with a unique mix of joy, optimism, and hit accessories.
Photographed by @annieleibovitz, Written by @christbollen, Styled by @jackborkett, Grooming by @jillianhalouska, Set Design by @maryhoward_setdesign, Produced by AL Studio. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Simone Rocha! Thom Browne! A Ralph Lauren return! The @PittiUomo_Official trade fair and @MilanFashionWeek Men’s show schedules this season are enhanced by some very exciting guest designers. At the link in bio, @Lucy_Maguire_ shares the brands and events to note.
Photo: @styledumonde
posts.alt_text: For decades, Indian artisans have quietly shaped European luxury, crafting the embroideries and textiles behind some of couture’s most iconic houses. This season marks a shift: more Indian designers than ever are on the official Paris calendar, with @ManishMalhotra05 set to make his Paris Haute Couture Week debut for @ManishMalhotraWorld on July 8 as the fourth Indian designer to feature on the schedule. @Sujstyle explores what these signals of change mean at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
As former model Chris Royer explained to Vogue, the best way to understand what it was like to work with Duane Michals is to use the Hungarian term kincsvadászat, which essentially means a “treasure hunt.” For Royer, a Duane Michals story was exactly that: “You were going on a trip, you knew you were going to discover things, it was a creative treasure hunt.” Michals who passed away on June 9th,  contributed regularly to Vogue during the 1970s and ’80s. His work during that period ranged from shooting stills of Robert Redford and Mia Farrow in costume on set while filming The Great Gatsby; to reportage of the San Francisco ballet and jewelry designer Elsa Peretti hard at work in her legendary studio-apartment; to portraits of notable men, including Yves Saint Laurent, Dudley Moore, and Philip Glass. Take a look back at his legacy at the link in bio.
1) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
2) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, February 1976
3) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, April 15, 1972
4) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
5) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976

posts.caption: While watching the 2026 NBA Finals at home, you may have noticed a dapper older gentleman in an outrageous suit among the stars. That would be @NYKnicks Legend Walt “Clyde” Frazier, who led the team to two championships in the 1970s. His singular approach to dressing has been carefully chronicled and graded (on a letter scale) by the site “ClydesoFly.com,” going back to 2013. Naturally, he doesn’t buy off the rack. Frazier’s suits were made by tailors in New York, mostly from fabric he plucked from shops in then Garment District and Lower East Side. Learn more about his dapper ensembles at the link in bio.

posts.caption: As we start to look for our warm-weather pieces, we’re asking ourselves, “How would the Olsen twins prepare for the season?” They would let proportion do the talking, with oversized cotton poplin blouses, languid trousers, and flouncy skirts. In addition to laid-back flops, they might also pull out an unexpected pair of crochet ballet flats or beaded sandals that embody the essence of the season. The key? Start with the basics and build from there. Tap the link in our bio to shop and recreate their chicest looks.

posts.caption: The #WorldCup has always been more than just a soccer tournament. While the games inspire frenzied fandom across the globe and transform star athletes into mega-celebs, they have also delivered their fair share of iconic fashion moments. Head to the link in bio to shop five stylish outfits we’d wear to the World Cup this summer.

posts.caption: One of the silver linings of being a frequent traveler—beyond the miles accrued—is that packing isn’t so much a chore as it is muscle memory. “I have it down to a science,” says Ilona Hamer, co-founder of Australian resortwear label Matteau of her dialed system. Last year alone, the stylist and consultant spent about 250 days on the go. At the link in bio, for the latest installment of #MyPackingStyle, Hamer shares what she packs for a holiday in the sun.

posts.caption: If summer’s sandals trends share a common thread, it’s a refusal to choose between any one aesthetic. Designers have traded the exclusively minimalist mood of recent summers for options that encourage self-expression. Whether it’s Le Monde Béryl’s pale blue beaded sandals, Gucci’s sporty heeled thongs, or Ancient Greek Sandals’ jelly flops, the chicest sandal trends of 2026 are personal rather than prescriptive. Discover them all at the link in bio.
